scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained initialized repository supplies one valid issue, task, specification, architecture, and document artifact in a single input batch.
required_observations: all five artifacts are routed to canonical kind paths
frontmatter kind and stored path agree after a consecutive update
no feature is created outside the C14 macro-feature contract

scenarios.task_args_template: --repo-root <contained-fixture-repo> と続けて --dry-run / --apply / 確認 --dry-run の 3 パスを同じ入力で回す (決定論 remote は fixture 内 .dev-graph/remote.json)
scenarios.fixture_contract: A deterministic GitHub remote-state fixture (.dev-graph/remote.json) has one valid import and one valid export, stable timestamps, IDs, aliases, and snapshots, so sync converges without touching any external GitHub API.
required_observations: the first sync applies the expected import and export
the second sync reports imports changes=0 and exports changes=0
stable IDs and snapshots are unchanged on the second run

scenarios.task_args_template: --repo-root <contained-fixture-repo> --feature-id F-LIVE-001 (package は fixture 内 system-plan/F-LIVE-001/ を skill が解決する)
scenarios.fixture_contract: The feature is confirmed/evaluation-pass/readiness-complete and the package is a valid P01..P13 exact-13 DAG with matching source digest and parent feature.
required_observations: a capability-build task-graph handoff is emitted for the exact-13 package
the handoff remains bound to the feature and source digest
the requirements skill generates no implementation source file

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained system-spec workspace and the declared system-spec-harness plugin provide all four required entry points, confirmed output, citations, and evaluator PASS.
required_observations: the declared system-spec-harness plugin is loaded and its canonical flow completes
the imported specification and architecture retain source lineage and evaluator evidence
registration occurs only through C02 and no duplicate elicitation or compile logic appears in dev-graph

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained git repository that is NOT yet dev-graph initialized, so that the first pass's creation of the six content roots, the routing policy and the graph store is attributable to the skill rather than to the fixture. Between the two passes the trial must edit one scaffolded canonical template and register one node through C02, so the second pass exercises non-overwrite against real user bytes and C11 cannot pass vacuously on an empty graph. The second pass must re-invoke the skill itself, not a hand-written existence check.
required_observations: the first pass creates the six content roots, the routing policy and the graph store
the second pass re-invokes the skill and reports zero planned changes
the template edited between the passes still holds its edited bytes after the second pass, proving non-overwrite by running init rather than by inspecting the file alone
the plugin hook source is resolved and its receipt or rejection diagnosis is recorded
config stores no absolute path and no token or node id
C11 passes against a graph that contains at least one node

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained initialized repository whose ready set exceeds --max-parallel and contains at least one overlapping resource_scope pair plus one active and one stale worktree lease, so batch splitting, conflict exclusion and lease expiry are all exercised rather than holding vacuously. At least one task must stay blocked by an unmet dependency.
required_observations: the overlapping resource_scope pair is never placed in the same batch
the blocked task is excluded from the ready set and the reason is reported
the active lease suppresses its task while the stale lease is reclaimed
batches respect --max-parallel and suggested_branch and worktree claim commands stay unique

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained initialized repository holds the macro decomposition input (the want) and deliberately holds no expected feature, architecture or task node, so every produced node is attributable to the skill rather than to the fixture. At least one produced feature must remain in pre-evaluation draft so the zero-issue-publication rule is exercised rather than holding vacuously.
required_observations: the produced feature and architecture nodes form an acyclic DAG whose inter-feature depends_on stays within the declared granularity threshold
pre-evaluation draft features publish zero issues on every binding
only features promoted to confirmed, evaluation-pass and readiness-complete become publication candidates
the dry run reports local, Beads, GitHub and Projects write counts of zero, and the preview graph is validated through the stdin path so no temporary file is created inside the managed repository

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained initialized repository holds two task nodes in the same lifecycle state with exactly one forward dependency between them, so ready and blocked can be told apart while the fixture does not pre-decide the aggregate the skill must compute.
required_observations: the reported status, closed_at and dependency edges equal the values stored in the graph store, compared field by field rather than by summary count
the dependent task is reported as blocked by its predecessor and the predecessor as ready
the run leaves graph, config, content and GitHub state unchanged

scenarios.fixture_contract: A contained initialized repository holds one feature with an exact-13 P01..P13 task set, a registration receipt carrying applied_count and expected_count, and a child set that is only partially completed, so the completed count and the applied count genuinely differ and the two halves of the progress reading cannot be confused with each other.
required_observations: the rendered HTML and CSS open with no additional runtime dependency and the SVG graph is displayed
the progress denominator equals the registration receipt applied_count and expected_count, which the renderer already refuses to render when they disagree
the progress numerator equals the number of child tasks whose status is done or closed, recomputed independently from the graph store rather than read back from the receipt
the rendered subject corresponds to the source_digest recorded in the registration receipt
